result:
Build is green (only warnings, no errors). No temporary instrumentation was added.

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/base.rs:763-767` (`load_fn`). The code attempted to default `mode` to `"bt"` via `opt_arg_string_bytes(3).unwrap_or_else(|_| b"bt".to_vec())`, but `opt_arg_string_bytes` returns `Ok(Vec::new())` (empty bytes) â€” never `Err` â€” when arg 3 is missing/nil. So `load(s)` with no mode arg got `mode=""`, which then failed `check_mode` in `do_.rs:1586` with `"attempt to load a text chunk (mode is '')"`. The failing test path: `checkload("for x do", "expected")` â†’ `load("for x do")` returns `(nil, "attempt to load a text chunk...")` â†’ `string.find(msg, "expected")` returns nil (no match) â†’ `assert(nil)` fires "assertion failed!".

**Fix:** Replaced the broken call with `state.opt_arg_string(3, b"bt")?`, which is the proper helper that takes a default and returns it for None/Nil args (already implemented at `state_stub.rs:435`).

**State after fix:** Verified manually that `load("for x do")` and `load("x:call")` now produce parser errors containing "expected" (e.g. `'=' or 'in' expected`). I did not re-run the full constructs.lua per instructions â€” the mega-loop will re-test. There is also a separate, smaller line-info off-by-one issue (the assert-failed location pointed at the function's def line instead of the assert call line), but it's not the cause of this failure and out of scope.
